reason: The Wave 3 Swift port bound every engine family (reducer, comparator, navigation, completion) to CrossyEngine and drained this list. Wave 15.1 rewrote the check contract to the attributed-majority vote flow (PROTOCOL.md sections 6, 10; DESIGN.md D32); the Swift port does not yet compute the vote state machine, so check is unbound and skipped-until-engine here, mirroring packages/engine/vectors.skip.json. The iOS engine rebind lands with Wave 15.5, which drains it from this list. A non-foreign family otherwise reappears here only if its engine binding is intentionally removed, which the per-family guard in Tests/VectorRunnerTests then flags. Mirrors packages/engine/vectors.skip.json.

foreign.reason: A foreign family's consumer is never CrossyEngine (PROTOCOL.md §13). client-store's consumer is apps/web's store (Wave 2.1d) and the iOS store (Phase I1b); clue-runs' consumer is the clue-run parser and renderer in apps/web and iOS. The runner discovers and shape-validates these vectors but never executes them; execution lives in apps/web's suite and the iOS suites. Unlike `families`, a foreign family is never bound to the engine and never leaves this list, so the Wave 3 per-family rebind that drains `families` must not touch it. Mirrors packages/engine/vectors.skip.json.
